channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Heat lose guard Dru Smith for remainder of season with torn Achilles
item.description: Miami Heat guard Dru Smith has suffered another season-ending injury, this one a torn Achilles in his left leg. Smith got hurt Monday night in Miami's 110-95 win over Brooklyn.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Wemby at The Garden. LeBron vs. Steph. The NBA's Christmas Day lineup, as always, has star power
item.description: LeBron James made his Christmas debut in 2003. Victor Wembanyama was born 10 days later. That's right: James has been featured on the NBA's big day for longer than Wembanyama has been alive.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Eight new WNBA coaches now in place. In 2025, 7 women and 6 men will be in charge on the bench
item.description: The WNBA now has all of its head coaching spots filled after there were eight open positions this offseason.
item.dc:creator: Doug Feinberg, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Winter Classic Recap: Six local teams finish 2-1
item.description: Now that school is out for Christmas break and high school basketball is on a hiatus until early 2025, let's recap a great weekend of hoops across the Grand Valley.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Penn State preparing for hard-charging Jeanty and Boise State in CFP quarterfinals
item.description: STATE COLLEGE, Pa. (AP) — Penn State players have watched Ashton Jeanty make opponents look silly all season. They don't want to be the next defenders Boise State’s star posterizes with jukes, spin moves, stiff arms and heavy shoulders.
item.dc:creator: TRAVIS JOHNSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Masters gets 10 more players from top 50 in the world ranking. Glover narrowly gets in
item.description: Lucas Glover held down the 50th spot in the final world ranking of the year, making him one of 10 players who will be added to the invitation list to play in the Masters. The field has eight more players than it did at this time a year ago.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cowboys beat Tampa Bay 26-24 as Baker Mayfield and the Bucs lose control of their playoff fate
item.description: ARLINGTON, Texas (AP) — Baker Mayfield came home to Texas looking to keep his Tampa Bay Buccaneers on track for the playoffs. Instead, they'll need some help.
item.dc:creator: SCHUYLER DIXON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Aggies bounce back with 75-68 win over St. Mary's
item.description: MORAGA, Calif. (ABC4 Sports) - Coming off its first loss of the season, the Utah State men’s basketball (11-1, 1-0 MW) got back in the win column Sunday night, closing out non-conference play with a 75-68 win over Saint Mary’s at the UCU Pavilion in Moraga.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Tiger's son makes the ace. Bernhard Langer makes the winning putt in playoff over Woods at PNC
item.description: Tiger Woods never had so much fun losing in a playoff. For the 67-year-old Bernhard Langer, it never gets old winning the PNC Championship with his son.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Bucs' Mayfield loses key member of offense before his Texas homecoming
item.description: The Tampa Bay Buccaneers have another difficult road test in their attempt to win the NFC South title.
item.dc:creator: Dan Lucas, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Lamar Jackson and the Ravens draw even atop the AFC North, beating the 1st-place Steelers 34-17
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Lamar Jackson was solid in Baltimore's biggest game of the season so far. Then, after one of the star quarterback's rare slip-ups, one of his teammates delivered the play of the day.
item.dc:creator: NOAH TRISTER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Day 3: Winter Classic Basketball
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX)—Palisade High School was buzzing on Day 3 of the Winter Classic as the Fruita Wildcats faced off against the Palmer Ridge Bears. Fruita entered the game riding high after a recent win against Ponderosa, while Palmer Ridge looked to snap a three-game losing streak.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: Jazz win back-to-back games for first time this season
item.description: BROOKLYN, N.Y. (ABC4 Sports) - For the first time since April, the Utah Jazz are on a winning streak. Lauri Markkanen scored 21 points, Collin Sexton and Svi Mykhailiuk each had 18 points, and the Utah Jazz beat the Brooklyn Nets 105-94 on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Northwestern demolishes DePaul in crosstown showdown
item.description: EVANSTON, Ill. (WGN) — An early blitz on the offensive end and playmaking down the stretch from Brooks Barnhizer and Nick Martinelli helped seal a convincing win for Northwestern over DePaul in the yearly crosstown showdown between the two programs.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Rickey Henderson, MLB legend, dies at 65: Reports
item.description: Rickey Henderson, the man who changed Major League Baseball with his blazing, unbeatable speed, has died, according to multiple reports.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, NewsNation

item.title: Pro pickleball players now earn more than WNBA, NWSL players
item.description: (KRON) -- The pickleball craze that's swept across the Bay Area and nation in recent years has apparently paid off, especially for female athletes. According to the Professional Pickleball Association, pro female players on the PPA Tour earned an average of $260,000 in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: 1 dead in small plane crash near home of NFL player Dion Dawkins
item.description: One person died in a small plane crash Thursday morning close to the house of Buffalo Bills offensive lineman Dion Dawkins, police said.
item.dc:creator: Aidan Joly, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bulls beat Celtics 117-108 in Boston
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Celtics forward Jaylen Brown took issue with back-to-back technical fouls called on coach Joe Mazzulla and himself late in the fourth quarter of Boston’s 117-108 loss to the Chicago Bulls on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Kucherov has 2 assists as Lightning beat Blues 3-1
item.description: Nikita Kucherov had a pair of assists to lead the Tampa Bay Lightning to a 3-1 victory against the St. Louis Blues. Anthony Cirelli, Gage Goncalves and Nick Perbix scored for the Lightning, who have won six of their last seven. Andrei Vasilevskiy finished with 31 saves in the win.
item.dc:creator: ERIK ERLENDSSON, WFLA, NBC

item.title: San Diego FC releases 2025 regular season schedule
item.description: San Diego FC will be kicking off its first regular season match in franchise history in February against a rival city.
item.dc:creator: Domenick Candelieri, Fox 5

item.title: Real Salt Lake's 2025 MLS season kicks off Feb. 22 with away game in San Jose
item.description: Real Salt Lake unveiled its entire 2025 MLS Season on Thursday, along with a few crucial dates such as the club's return to the CONCACAF Champions Cup.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, ABC 4

item.title: Boys Basketball: La Jolla 55, Army-Navy 47
item.description: SAN DIEGO (KUSI)- Boys hoops in La Jolla with the Vikings hosting Army-Navy from Carlsbad. Great game that went right down to the wire, La Jolla won 52-47. Vikings were lead by Leo Hawkinson with 16. Warriors were lead by Yanler Ramos game high 26 points.
item.dc:creator: Fox 5

item.title: NCAA president says there are 'less than 10' transgender athletes in college sports
item.description: NCAA President Charlie Baker told a Senate panel that there are fewer than 10 transgender athletes he is aware of who currently compete in college sports, pouring cold water on an issue Republicans have said is a nationwide problem and one that is increasingly fraught territory for Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, WGN 9

item.title: Government funding deal opens door to move Washington Commanders stadium to DC
item.description: The Washington Commanders could soon be kicking off in the heart of the nation’s capital. The government funding deal unveiled by congressional negotiators Tuesday paved the way for moving the Commanders' stadium from Maryland to Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation
